fixed_router_packed_sim_ceiling.artifact_read: metrics.md retained-path resample, fixed-router packed-sim ceiling
fixed_router_packed_sim_ceiling.caveat: Fixed-router packed-sim is useful as a math/perf ceiling but does not cover real learned-routing training behavior.

learned_router_packed_sim_bridge.caveat: This bridge has real router gradients but remains a PyTorch packed-sim path with padded expert batches.

updated_attention_li_diagnostic_floor.read: This is a cross-artifact diagnostic floor, not a directly integrated full-layer measurement. It combines the TorchTitan-shape shared-KV attention boundary fwd+bwd with the separate unfused LI auxiliary-loss pipeline after the wide LI tile retune.

why_not_close: The best fixed-router packed-sim full layer is still 2.74x slower than the 24.9 ms single-layer target.
The learned-router bridge is 3.11x slower than the 24.9 ms target.
The updated shared-KV attention plus LI diagnostic floor is already 27.85 ms before MoE, residual/RMSNorm, full compressor backward integration, and layer bookkeeping.
next_single_gpu_actions: Fuse the LI side pipeline into sparse MLA/compressor backward so target_probs, derivative, and LI dQ/dK/dW are not separate side launches/tensors.
Continue reducing the shared-KV backward boundary; the retained S4096 attention boundary is 23.36 ms fwd+bwd and leaves too little budget for the full layer.
Replace padded PyTorch MoE expert batches with compact-native grouped training GEMM/backward; learned-router S4096 is 77.44 ms and S2048 attribution still shows routed MoE dgrad/wgrad as the limiter.
